title: Puss in Boots
content:
When an old miller died, he left his three sons everything he owned. The oldest got the mill, the middle son got the donkey, and the youngest got only the cat. The young man sighed sadly. "What use is a cat?"

But the cat sat up and said, "Do not despair, master. Give me a fine pair of boots and a bag, and I will make your fortune." The young man was astonished, but he bought the boots.

Puss put on his boots and walked tall. He used his bag to catch rabbits and pheasants and brought them to the king as gifts. "These come from my master, the noble Marquis of Carabas," he said, bowing elegantly.

The king was pleased and asked to meet this marquis. Puss hurried to his master. "Jump in the river when the royal carriage passes!" When the king passed, Puss shouted that the Marquis had been robbed. The kind king sent fine clothes at once.

The young man rode in the carriage looking like a true lord. Puss ran ahead and told every farmer to say their fields belonged to the Marquis of Carabas. They did, and the king was greatly impressed.

Then Puss arrived at a huge castle owned by a terrifying ogre. "I have heard you can turn into any creature," said Puss boldly. "Can you become a mighty lion?" The vain ogre roared and became a lion. Puss was frightened but stood firm.

"Impressive! But could you shrink into something tiny, like a mouse?" The ogre shrank into a small mouse at once. Puss pounced! When the king arrived, Puss welcomed him into the castle as the home of the Marquis. The king gave his daughter in marriage, and the clever cat had made his master's fortune just as he had promised.

author: Charles Perrault
